# S003 Run 004

## Metadata

- Scenario ID: `S003`
- Scenario: `S003-async-gpu-remote-node-image`
- Status: pass-with-findings
- Coordinator: Codex
- Executed: June 5, 2026
- Workspace: `contract-sim/workspaces/S003/`

## Purpose

Restart S003 after Run 003 fixes to verify whether the implementation-mirrored
workspace can drive the full async GPU image-generation happy path without
giving actors sibling folders, legacy roles, OpenAPI, or the full requirements
tree.

Each actor received only its local workspace folder plus the S003 scenario.
Actors were forbidden from editing files or inventing missing behavior.

## Constraint Gate

Run 004 used changes approved by a read-only constraint validator in Run 003:

- Concrete execution-plan fixtures across C05, C04, and the runner.
- Provider content handoff fixtures with size, checksum, and binary headers.
- C07 upload, content, complete, list, metadata, and content fixtures.
- C04 public logs and artifact-content behavior.
- `producer_ref` as the canonical job-artifact correlation field, with
  `owner_subject_id` kept separate for policy.
- C08 envelope consistency, mandatory owner context, and per-candidate
  discovery authorization.
- C09 health/runtime fixtures and service initial state.
- Agent-facing action-link consistency.

## Actor Results

| Actor | Status | Structure Check | Result |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `agent-user` | pass-with-findings | pass | Completed discovery, async invocation, job observation, logs, artifact list, and binary content retrieval from public contracts only. |
| `c04-agent-tool-gateway` | pass-with-findings | partial | Completed discovery, invocation, job projection, logs, artifacts, and binary proxy behavior; needs explicit observe/read policy and content route in inbound API list. |
| `c03-service-catalog` | pass-with-findings | pass | Completed catalog list, route lookup, cursor handling for S003, and unknown route error. |
| `c08-access-policy-and-secrets` | pass-with-findings | pass | Completed auth, discovery, invoke, owner-scoped job/artifact checks, worker checks, and missing-context deny. |
| `c05-async-job-service` | pass-with-findings | pass | Completed create, claim, heartbeat, explicit running transition, logs, complete, fail, invalid transition, and agent-safe projection. |
| `c06-resource-lease-service` | pass-with-findings | pass | Completed lease grant, heartbeat, release, pending queue, status/cancel, and failure-code coverage. |
| `c07-artifact-store` | pass-with-findings | partial | Completed upload create/content/complete, metadata, list by `producer_ref`, content read, and validation-error branches. |
| `c09-runtime-node-agent` | pass-with-findings | pass | Completed node health, stopped-to-running lifecycle, idempotent start/stop, and runtime error branches. |
| `c10-comfyui-provider` | pass-with-findings | pass | Completed health, validation, blocking invoke, runner-only content ref, content retrieval, forbidden agent access, unknown ref, and expiry behavior. |
| `composition-runner` | pass-with-findings | partial | Completed full internal sequence from job claim through C07 upload and C05 completion; needs local C06 heartbeat/release fixtures. |

## Main Result

The S003 happy path is no longer blocked.

The agent can complete the public workflow without knowing leases, runtime node
details, route metadata, provider endpoints, provider content refs, storage
paths, or sibling component state.

The runner can complete the internal workflow while keeping provider
`content_ref` transient and storing only final C07 artifact IDs in C05.

## Findings

| Finding ID | Category | Severity | Status | Target | Short Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `S003-R004-F001` | `CONTRACT_COMPLETENESS` | medium | open | Agent / C04 / C05 | Agent-visible non-terminal job examples are missing, so polling jumps directly to `succeeded`. |
| `S003-R004-F002` | `CONTRACT_COMPLETENESS` | low | open | Agent / C04 / C05 | Logs pagination request examples lack explicit `cursor` and `limit` rules in local packages. |
| `S003-R004-F003` | `BOUNDARY_PRESSURE` | medium | open | Agent / C04 / C05 | Public logs expose `fields.provider: svc_comfyui_gpu`; requirements must decide whether provider/service IDs are public in logs. |
| `S003-R004-F004` | `CONTRACT_CONSISTENCY` | low | open | Agent / C04 / C07 | Artifact items include both `download_link` and `links.content`; raw link fields need an explicit exception or removal. |
| `S003-R004-F005` | `CONTRACT_COMPLETENESS` | low | open | Agent / C04 / C07 | Binary artifact content errors are not shown in agent-local context. |
| `S003-R004-F006` | `CONTRACT_COMPLETENESS` | medium | open | C04 | `GET /v1/artifacts/{artifact_id}/content` is required for C04 proxy behavior but missing from C04 inbound public API list. |
| `S003-R004-F007` | `SECURITY_TRUST` | high | open | C04 / C08 | Job status, logs, artifact list, and artifact content authorization rules are underspecified in C04 local context. |
| `S003-R004-F008` | `CONTRACT_COMPLETENESS` | medium | open | C04 / C05 | C05 job status projection fixture lacks an explicit request target and full envelope. |
| `S003-R004-F009` | `PUBLIC_API` | medium | open | C04 | Invoke idempotency is implied by link metadata but missing from the request example and conflict behavior. |
| `S003-R004-F010` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | medium | open | C03 | Invalid or stale catalog cursor behavior is not specified. |
| `S003-R004-F011` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | low | open | C03 | Exact list-filter miss behavior for `capability_id` is not explicit. |
| `S003-R004-F012` | `BOUNDARY_CLARITY` | medium | open | C03 / C04 | Catalog list responses include route metadata; requirements should state this is component-facing and C04 must project agent-safe tool records. |
| `S003-R004-F013` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | medium | open | C08 | Worker action required context is thinner than owner-scoped context, especially lease actions. |
| `S003-R004-F014` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | low | open | C08 | Invalid credential parse behavior can return `valid:false` or `unauthorized`; boundary needs a deterministic rule. |
| `S003-R004-F015` | `BOUNDARY_PRESSURE` | medium | open | C08 / C04 | Owner-scoped checks depend on caller-supplied owner context; C04 must provide owner context from public projections without C08 reading C05/C07 internals. |
| `S003-R004-F016` | `LIFECYCLE_GAP` | medium | open | C05 | Claim conflict, stale-claim, worker mismatch, and terminal ownership checks are not defined. |
| `S003-R004-F017` | `SCHEMA_GAP` | low | open | C05 | Heartbeat refresh response omits refreshed claim `expires_at`. |
| `S003-R004-F018` | `BOUNDARY_PRESSURE` | medium | open | C05 | Execution plan carries provider/node route data in C05; this remains acceptable only as a documented worker-visible projection. |
| `S003-R004-F019` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | medium | open | C06 | C06 error envelopes need HTTP status, canonical body fields, and retryability. |
| `S003-R004-F020` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | medium | open | C06 | Canceled lease request response shape is underspecified. |
| `S003-R004-F021` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | low | open | C06 | Heartbeat expiration math is implied but not stated. |
| `S003-R004-F022` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | medium | open | C06 | Queue grant-on-release behavior is not fully defined. |
| `S003-R004-F023` | `FIXTURE_GAP` | medium | open | C07 / C10 / runner | The 68-byte PNG is named but no local byte fixture file exists. |
| `S003-R004-F024` | `CONTRACT_AMBIGUITY` | low | open | C07 | Upload create status is not explicit. |
| `S003-R004-F025` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | medium | open | C07 | Standard error envelope examples are missing from the C07 package. |
| `S003-R004-F026` | `CONTRACT_AMBIGUITY` | low | open | C07 | Duplicate completion has two possible outcomes without a precise decision rule. |
| `S003-R004-F027` | `TEST_CANDIDATE` | low | open | C09 | C09 fixture README names fixtures but does not include concrete JSON files. |
| `S003-R004-F028` | `LIFECYCLE_CLARITY` | medium | open | C09 | `starting` is allowed but lacks a concrete response example and polling timing rule. |
| `S003-R004-F029` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | medium | open | C10 | C10 success envelopes for health/invoke are not included locally. |
| `S003-R004-F030` | `SECURITY_TRUST` | high | open | C10 | Runner auth/scope fields for provider content retrieval are missing. |
| `S003-R004-F031` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | medium | open | C10 | Exact error bodies for `not_found`, `forbidden`, and `provider_unavailable` are missing. |
| `S003-R004-F032` | `LIFECYCLE_GAP` | low | open | C10 | Content-ref expiry inclusivity and cleanup semantics are underspecified. |
| `S003-R004-F033` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | medium | open | Runner / C06 | Runner lacks local C06 heartbeat endpoint/response fixture. |
| `S003-R004-F034` | `CONTRACT_GAP` | medium | open | Runner / C06 | Runner lacks local C06 release endpoint/response fixture. |
| `S003-R004-F035` | `TEST_CANDIDATE` | low | open | Runner / C09 | Runner has only service-running fixture and cannot test start-required behavior. |

## Positive Signals

- The whole happy path ran to completion.
- The filesystem layout is now flushing out future code/test organization.
- No actor required sibling folders or full requirements context.
- C04 preserved agent-safe projections.
- C08 denied missing owner context instead of reading C05/C07 internals.
- C10 kept provider content refs runner-only and transient.
- C07 stayed the durable owner of final artifact metadata, bytes, and public retrieval.
- The runner completed C05 -> C06 -> C09 -> C10 -> C07 -> C05 without leaking
  provider refs into final job state.

## Proposed Fix Batch For Constraint Validation

Before editing requirements or role packages again, validate this proposed
batch:

1. Add explicit C04 inbound content route and observe/read policy checks:
   `job.read` for status/logs, `artifact.read` for artifact list/content, with
   required `owner_subject_id` context.
2. Add C08 local policy fixtures and required context rules for observe/read
   actions, worker lease actions, and deterministic invalid-credential parse
   behavior.
3. Add C10 runner auth/scope requirements for provider content retrieval,
   preserving `content_ref` as runner-only, opaque, expiring, and unavailable
   to agents.
4. Add C10 success envelopes and canonical `not_found`, `forbidden`, and
   `provider_unavailable` error envelopes.
5. Add C06 heartbeat/release response fixtures to the runner package and C06
   package, plus canceled request response, error envelopes, heartbeat
   expiration math, and queue grant-on-release behavior.
6. Add an ASCII base64 byte fixture for `provider_png_s003_0001` in local C07,
   C10, and runner fixture folders, with size and checksum, but do not expose
   fixture ids as public wire fields.
7. Add C07 upload create status, standard error envelopes, and deterministic
   duplicate-complete/idempotency rules.
8. Add C05 claim conflict, stale claim, worker mismatch, heartbeat expiry,
   and terminal ownership rules; keep execution plan documented as
   worker-visible only.
9. Add non-terminal agent job examples, logs cursor request examples, and
   binary content error examples to agent/C04 packages.
10. Decide that provider/service identifiers are not public in S003 logs and
    remove or redact them from agent-facing log fixtures unless explicitly
    reclassified as public diagnostics.
11. Clarify that C03 catalog list with route metadata is component-facing;
    C04 must project agent-safe `Tool` records. Also define stale cursor and
    list-filter miss behavior.
12. Add C09 `starting` example, polling timing rule, and concrete service-start
    fixture for runner start-required path.

## Constraint Validation For Proposed Fix Batch

- Validator: read-only constraint validator subagent.
- Outcome: `approved-with-notes`.
- Objections: none.
- Debate/arbitration required: no.
- Notes:
  - Apply the batch narrowly as contract and fixture clarification.
  - C04 may expose artifact content only as an agent-facing gateway/proxy over
    C07 public retrieval. C04 must not own artifact bytes, artifact storage, or
    provider-local refs.
  - C08 policy fixtures must deny when `owner_subject_id` is missing. C08 must
    not infer ownership by reading C05 or C07 state.
  - Provider `content_ref` must remain runner-only, opaque, expiring, non-path,
    unavailable to agents, and not a replacement for C07 upload sessions.
  - C05 execution plans may appear only in worker-visible claim/projection
    fixtures.
  - PNG byte fixtures are local public-contract fixtures only; fixture ids are
    not wire fields, durable artifact ids, provider refs, or shared filesystem
    paths.
  - C06 lease lifecycle fixes must treat `requester_id` as opaque.
  - Provider/service ids should be redacted from agent-facing logs for S003
    unless a separate design decision classifies them as public diagnostics.
  - C03 route metadata remains component-facing. C04 must project separate
    agent-safe `Tool` records.

## Outcome

Pass with findings. The role-play has advanced from broad actor-context gaps to
specific security, lifecycle, and fixture precision issues.
